content: The performance gap between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and dedicated curious agents can be explained by underutilized structured memory optimized for active learning: 2024-2025 SOTA active agent memory systems explicitly sort novel vs. familiar experience, and this capability can resolve the longstanding ambiguity in how curiosity signals are prioritized for exploration. This leads to the testable hypothesis that retrofitting state-of-the-art active memory architectures into standard ICM/RND frameworks will outperform both vanilla ICM/RND and hand-designed curious agents on long-horizon sparse-reward tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
